_meta.keyKnowledge: We play together with other people, sharing and taking turns.

_source.w: We play together with other people, sharing and taking turns.
y: Playing together teaches us to cooperate and share.
Associative play is where we learn the basics of friendship.
Interacting through play builds communication and social skills.
eg: We share materials during a group activity.
We take turns on the swing or with a popular toy.
We join in a group game and follow the simple rules.
We play a role in a pretend game with other children.
We build something together using blocks or construction materials.
We include someone who is playing alone.
